mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which reduces the present value of equities and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns turns positive and exceeds 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with an elevated federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises recession risk, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a Rank IC below -0.04 over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during a post-recession recovery signals demand normalization and capacity utilization improvements that drive earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of quarters when GDPC1 accelerates during post-recession recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect positive supply-side momentum and improving manufacturing profitability that translate into near-term earnings optimism, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following month-over-month INDPRO increases is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units signal a contraction in interest-rate-sensitive durable demand that precedes broader economic weakening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of observations when HOUST is below 1,500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and a rise in the unemployment rate constitute a joint recession signal that amplifies downside risk to corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO changes and UNRATE changes fails to achieve a Rank IC below -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent exerts a nonlinear drag on equity valuations through elevated discount rates and tightened financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent.
